question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `getStreamType()` hardcodes `StreamType.RECORD` to craft a scenario where the cutover service is tricked into keeping the record stream active past the intended cutover point, preventing the more tamper-resistant block stream verification from being applied to new transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a Kubernetes ConfigMap or environment variable injection vulnerability to set `HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_DOWNLOADER_RECORD_ENABLED=false`, disabling record stream ingestion so that tampered transactions submitted during the disabled window are never recorded in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set `CommonDownloaderProperties.consensusRatio=0` (minimum allowed by `@Min(0)`), causing the downloader to accept a record file from zero nodes as meeting consensus, allowing a completely fabricated record file with tampered transactions to be ingested as valid?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set `CommonDownloaderProperties.downloadRatio=0` (minimum allowed by `@Min(0)` with no `@NotNull`), causing the downloader to download record files from zero nodes, effectively skipping all transaction record fetching and allowing tampered transactions to go undetected?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `allowAnonymousAccess` flag in `CommonDownloaderProperties` to enable anonymous access to the record file bucket, then use that anonymous access to enumerate and download record files containing transaction details that should be protected?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate `CommonDownloaderProperties.cloudProvider` to `LOCAL` (SourceType.LOCAL with empty endpoint), causing the downloader to read record files from the local filesystem instead of the legitimate cloud source, and then exploit a file write vulnerability to place tampered record files at the expected local path?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user enable `writeFiles=true` via config manipulation, causing record files to be written to a predictable filesystem path, and then exploit a symlink attack to redirect the write to overwrite a critical system file or another record file with tampered transaction content?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted `accessKey` and `secretKey` into `CommonDownloaderProperties` via environment variables, causing the downloader to authenticate to an attacker-controlled S3-compatible endpoint that serves tampered record files with valid-looking signatures?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set `CommonDownloaderProperties.region` to a region where the attacker controls an S3 bucket with the same name as the legitimate bucket, causing the downloader to fetch tampered record files from the attacker's bucket due to region-based bucket resolution?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user set `frequency` to `null` (bypassing the `@NotNull` constraint via a config server that sends a null value), causing a NullPointerException in the `@Scheduled` expression `#{@recordDownloaderProperties.getFrequency().toMillis()}` that crashes the scheduler thread and permanently halts record file ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `gcpProjectId` field in `CommonDownloaderProperties` to redirect GCP-based record file downloads to an attacker-controlled GCS bucket, causing tampered transaction records to be ingested as legitimate consensus output?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition in the `CutoverServiceImpl.isActive()` check that reads `recordDownloaderProperties.isEnabled()` without synchronization on the property itself, causing a TOCTOU window where the enabled state is toggled between the check and the actual download, allowing tampered record files to be processed during the inconsistent state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set `CommonDownloaderProperties.batchSize=1`, causing the downloader to process only one record file per cycle, dramatically slowing transaction ingestion and creating a large backlog window during which tampered transactions submitted to the network are not yet reflected in the mirror node, enabling double-spend or replay attacks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate `CommonDownloaderProperties.sources` to an empty list (bypassing the `@Valid` constraint), causing the `init()` method to add a default source with no credentials pointing to an attacker-controlled endpoint, resulting in tampered record files being downloaded?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `getStreamType()` returns `StreamType.RECORD` to craft a scenario where the `DateRangeCalculator` uses stale date range filters for the RECORD stream type, causing the downloader to skip over a range of record files containing tampered transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `persistBytes=false` default to ensure that after tampered record files are ingested, no raw byte evidence is stored in the database, making it impossible to retroactively verify the integrity of processed transactions even if the tampering is later suspected?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `pathRefreshInterval` minimum of 1 second to set it to exactly 1 second, causing extremely frequent address book refreshes that create a race condition between the node list update and the record file download, allowing tampered files from a node that was just removed from the address book to be accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set `CommonDownloaderProperties.consensusRatio` to exactly `1/3` (the default) while simultaneously reducing the total number of tracked nodes via address book manipulation, so that only a single compromised node's tampered record file is needed to meet the consensus threshold?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a high volume of transactions to the Hedera network that cause the record file downloader to fall behind, and then exploit the `enabled` flag toggle to create a gap in record file processing where tampered transactions are submitted and never ingested by the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `endpointOverride` field in `CommonDownloaderProperties` by injecting a Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) payload as the endpoint URL, causing the downloader to make requests to internal network services or metadata endpoints that leak credentials used to authenticate to the legitimate record file source?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set `CommonDownloaderProperties.endpointOverride` to an HTTP (non-TLS) URL, causing record file downloads to occur over an unencrypted channel where a man-in-the-middle attacker can substitute tampered record files in transit?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user set `frequency` to a negative Duration value (e.g., `PT-1S`), which passes the `@NotNull` constraint but may cause undefined behavior in the `@Scheduled` fixed delay calculation, potentially causing the scheduler to run continuously without delay and exhaust resources needed for signature verification of transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@Min(2L)` constraint on `maxSize` to set it to exactly 2 bytes, causing all record files (which are always larger than 2 bytes) to be rejected as exceeding the maximum size, permanently halting transaction ingestion and preventing detection of tampered transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate `CommonDownloaderProperties.pathType` to `AUTO`, causing the downloader to attempt automatic path detection that may resolve to an attacker-controlled path if the auto-detection logic can be influenced by crafted responses from the storage backend?
